title: Wiki homes mirror memory homes: public store → committed wiki/, full graph → overlay-repo wiki (--private)
context: The first wiki cut rendered PUBLIC-store records only — correct for committed pages, but owners with a private overlay (or shared mode, where the overlay IS the store) got a wiki blind to most of their graph. Requirement: the wiki must be able to read the private overlay when chosen, and a private wiki must live in the private repo.
decision: A WikiHome pairs SOURCE with DESTINATION so overlay-inclusive reads can only ever land in the overlay repo: publicHome = public-store reads (store.json) → <repo>/wiki/ + committed .hunch/wiki-manifest.json; privateHome (`hunch wiki --private`, requires a configured overlay) = full-union reads (store.recs) → <overlay-repo-root>/wiki/ + manifest inside the overlay's .hunch/ (pages sit BESIDE the overlay .hunch, keeping it memory-JSON-only for the auto-commit guard). Everything else — packHash freshness, wiki-stale drift (computeWikiDrift now checks BOTH adopted homes; private findings are labeled and only exist where the overlay is configured, so CI runners never see them), topic pins, --heal — is home-agnostic. Committed grounding docs (wikiSummary → CLAUDE.md et al) advertise the PUBLIC wiki only, and refreshExistingGrounding is skipped for private runs: committed docs must not reveal a private wiki exists. The private index page carries a do-not-publish banner.
consequences: Two independent manifests/ledgers; the same component can be fresh publicly and stale privately (and vice versa)
hunch drift on a maintainer machine reports both homes (private findings labeled '(private overlay wiki)'); CI without HUNCH_PRIVATE_DIR/local.json reports public only, by construction
Shared-mode teams get a full-graph wiki in the team overlay repo via --private; the committed public wiki stays the curated demo surface
assemblePack gained a source parameter (public|all) — the leak boundary is now explicit at the query layer and locked by leak-check tests
alternatives_rejected: An --include-private flag writing union pages into the committed wiki/: one un-reviewed `git add` from leaking the overlay — source and destination must be one choice, not two flags
Auto-selecting the private home whenever an overlay is configured: silent output-location change; --private is explicit like every other overlay behavior
Pages inside the overlay's .hunch/ dir: the overlay auto-commit guard requires .hunch to stay memory-JSON-only; markdown there would make every capture commit refuse
Advertising the private wiki in committed grounding docs: existence of a private store is itself information — wikiSummary stays public-manifest-only
